knowledge_gaps: Market pricing of fiscal dominance risk
The 1946-1980 'beautiful deleveraging' decomposes approximately as 40% inflation, 35% growth, 15% surplus, 10% repression.
SEC Treasury clearing mandate (CMESC Q2 2026) could serve as a Minsky moment catalyst for basis trade deleveraging.
Biofuel mandates (36% of corn to ethanol) create a structural inflation ratchet under fiscal dominance via farm-state political economy.
ON RRP depletion has fundamentally altered crisis propagation plumbing.
Gold/copper ratio breaking its traditional negative correlation could serve as a fiscal dominance regime indicator.
AI productivity growth (0.5-1.0pp additional real GDP) could invalidate the entire r-g framework and prevent fiscal dominance activation.
Both primary risk-off hedges (long UST + long USD) could fail simultaneously under US-origin fiscal stress (Dollar Smile bifurcation).
No democracy has sustained intentional financial repression for >15 years without an external anchor or authoritarian governance.
BoJ normalization creates a cross-border contagion channel via Japanese Treasury repatriation (~$1.1T).
Exporter stocks-to-use (not global) is the relevant vulnerability metric for agricultural supply shocks, with non-linear price response at ~16% threshold.
focus.instruction: This topic has been studied at overview level. Focus on: (1) specific subtopics listed, (2) closing identified knowledge gaps, (3) cross-topic interactions with connected topics, (4) edge cases and regime-dependent behavior.
